select_when: Consistency, standardization, or deep commitment is creating compounding advantage, so the real question is which path should become sticky.
A migration or platform decision is being treated as reversible even though dependency drag, retraining, or coexistence costs are already growing.
Switching looks cheap in slides, but local habits, contracts, or workflows are quietly narrowing the option set over time.
The team needs to distinguish beneficial commitment from inertia-driven persistence.
avoid_when: The situation is genuinely novel, so reusing the incumbent frame would likely become an unhelpful or misleading default.
The model is being used to defend inherited architecture or belief simply because departure feels costly, disloyal, or identity-threatening.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on dependency drag, compounding reversal cost, and deliberate stickiness rather than on generic consistency rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor migration-policy sprawl because the raw source is already explicit on coexistence costs, reversal narrowing, and deliberate commitment.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains Wave 1 to Wave 3 only.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans cognitive schemas, platform migration, and organizational habit formation.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that lock-in matters most when stickiness compounds advantage or quietly narrows later switching options.

avoid_when.note: The novelty warning is explicit in the source; the identity-threat phrasing is conservative reviewed normalization of the danger-when block.

input_type.note: The raw model activates on path dependence and reversibility problems, but the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields a clearer read on stickiness and reversal cost; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Lock-in clearly works through counterfactual reversal thinking and system-path effects, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
